ingredients.entityContext: One of the convenience stores in the Yoyogi area that appears in the film. The 7-Eleven chain recurs throughout the story as Hodaka's source of cheap meals during his early days in Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Japan National Stadium
ingredients.address: 10-1 Kasumigaokamachi, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shinjuku Olympic Stadium is seen fully submerged in water in the film's climactic vision of a flooded Tokyo. The image of the iconic arena beneath the waterline is one of Weathering With You's most striking frames and a defining visual of the film's alternate Tokyo.

ingredients.address: 1-chōme-17 Higashitabata, Kita City, Tokyo 114-0013, Japan

ingredients.address: 1 Chome-28-2 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The covered shopping street near Ikebukuro Station that appears in background scenes, part of the dense northern Tokyo streetscape the film uses alongside Shinjuku.

ingredients.name: Atami Building
ingredients.address: Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The real building in Shinjuku that served as the model for Keisuke Suga's cramped magazine office and apartment, the base of operations for Hodaka's job and the setting for several key scenes with the ragtag team.

ingredients.entityContext: The Odaiba observation point with its view of Tokyo Bay and the Rainbow Bridge, used in the film's wide exterior shots of the city from the waterfront, establishing the scale of Tokyo's flooded future.

ingredients.address: 4 Chome-6-16 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-8212, Japan

ingredients.name: Daiichi Inn Ikebukuro
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-42-8 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The budget hotel in Ikebukuro where Hodaka stays during his early, precarious days in Tokyo, and one of the documented lodging locations from the film's pilgrimage guides.

ingredients.name: Takeshiba Ship Terminal
ingredients.type: フェリー サービス
categories: フェリー サービス
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-16-1 Kaigan, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The ferry terminal where Hodaka Morishima arrives in Tokyo at the opening of the film, stepping off the boat from his island home and beginning his life in the city. It is the starting point of the entire story.

ingredients.name: Akebonobashi
ingredients.address: 7 Sumiyoshichō, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 162-0065, Japan

ingredients.entityContext: The riverside park in Koto City along the Tatekawa river canal, confirmed as one of the background outdoor locations the film recreates as part of its detailed eastern Tokyo geography.

ingredients.address: 3 Chome-4-15 Mejiro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0031, Japan

ingredients.address: 1-chōme Kabukichō, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The neon-lit avenue at the heart of Kabukicho that appears repeatedly in the film as Hodaka wanders through Shinjuku's entertainment district, and one of the primary streets in the film's Shinjuku streetscape.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Shinjuku Kabukicho Ichibangai
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 新宿歌舞伎町一番街店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-16-1 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: One of the Kabukicho convenience stores that appears in the film as part of the district's streetscape. Hodaka frequently relies on convenience stores for food during his precarious early days in Shinjuku.

ingredients.name: Ikebukuro Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-1 Ikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge in Ikebukuro that features in scenes set in the northern Tokyo district. Hodaka moves through both Shinjuku and Ikebukuro repeatedly, and the bridge is a confirmed location in pilgrimage guides.

ingredients.name: Tokyo City View
ingredients.localName: 六本木ヒルズ展望台 東京シティビュー
ingredients.address: Roppongi Hills Mori Tower, 52階, 6 Chome-10-1 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Roppongi Hills observation deck featured in the film's high-altitude cityscape shots, used to convey the sweeping, cloud-level perspective central to the story's weather and sky imagery.

ingredients.name: Eidai Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒135-0034 Tokyo, Koto City, Eitai, 1 Chome−31, ～中央区新川１丁目
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge over the Sumida River in Koto City that appears in the film's exterior Tokyo scenes, and one of the waterway crossings documented in pilgrimage guides for the film.

ingredients.name: Nishishinjuku Prime Square
ingredients.localName: 西新宿プライムスクエア
ingredients.address: Japan

ingredients.name: Hachikan Shrine
ingredients.address: 8F, 8 Chome-4-5 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: One of the small Tokyo shrines that appears in the film's background imagery. Makoto Shinkai's films characteristically feature small neighbourhood shrines as quiet counterpoints to the urban setting.

ingredients.name: McDonald's Seibu Shinjuku Station Store
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド 西武新宿駅前店
ingredients.type: ファースト フード店
categories: ファースト フード店
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-24-1 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The McDonald's near Seibu-Shinjuku Station that is the model for the restaurant where Hina Amano works part-time, and one of the most-documented pilgrimage stops where the uniforms and layout match the film almost shot-for-shot.

ingredients.name: Kaminarimon
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Asakusa gate appears in a key scene from the film, part of Shinkai's meticulous recreation of real Tokyo landmarks in the alternate-weather version of the city.

ingredients.name: Haruka Observatory
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒100-0601 Tokyo, Kozushima Village

ingredients.name: Tokyo Teleport Station
ingredients.address: 1-chōme-2 Aomi, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0064, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Odaiba monorail station that appears in scenes set on the artificial island, part of the film's depiction of Tokyo Bay area locations distinct from the Shinjuku-heavy main setting.

ingredients.name: Sumida River Terrace
ingredients.address: 1-11 Nihonbashinakasu, Chuo City, Tokyo 103-0008, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The riverside walkway along the Sumida River that appears in the film's exterior scenes, part of the broader Tokyo geography the film traces as Hodaka moves across the city.

ingredients.address: 1 Chome-17 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic gate marking the entrance to Kabukicho that features in multiple scenes as Hodaka moves through Shinjuku, a recognised landmark in the film's depiction of the district.

ingredients.address: 3 Chome-8-35 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The open park area in Koto City that serves as one of the film's exterior green-space locations, part of the diverse Tokyo geography Shinkai mapped across the city for Weathering With You.

ingredients.name: JR Tokyo General Hospital
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-3 Yoyogi, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-8528, Japan

ingredients.name: Cine City Square
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-20 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The open square in front of Shinjuku's Cine City complex that appears in the film as part of the Shinjuku streetscape, and one of the recognisable outdoor spaces in the Kabukicho/Shinjuku area the film meticulously recreates.

ingredients.name: Shiba Park
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-8 Shibakoen, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0011, Japan

ingredients.address: Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous intersection appears in the film's Tokyo cityscape sequences, used to establish the scale and energy of the city as Hodaka navigates his new life, and one of the most-photographed locations in any Makoto Shinkai film set in Tokyo.

ingredients.entityContext: The park in Koto City near Tokyo Bay that appears in the film's waterfront scenes, part of the eastern Tokyo geography the film uses to contrast with the Shinjuku/Ikebukuro core.
